title: #eng-runtime: 5832109999
preview:
Priya Nair: quick sync — tradeoffs between per-tenant private queues vs a single shared backlog for continuous batching. I sketched two proposals and want quick reactions: 1) explicit per-tenant queue objects with quotas/headroom; 2) shared backlog with budget hints to admission. thoughts?

Ethan Cole: +1 to evaluat...
text:
Priya Nair: quick sync — tradeoffs between per-tenant private queues vs a single shared backlog for continuous batching. I sketched two proposals and want quick reactions: 1) explicit per-tenant queue objects with quotas/headroom; 2) shared backlog with budget hints to admission. thoughts?

Ethan Cole: +1 to evaluating budget hints. Per-tenant queues feel simpler for isolation but explode resource tracking.

Samir Gupta: my worry with per-tenant queues is long-tail memory blowup when many idle tenants have pinned structures. We saw this in loadgen. Shared backlog + soft budgets gives better headroom packing.

Marta Lopez: can we combine? ephemeral per-tenant handles that are created on demand and retired after idle timeout. keep a compact index into shared backlog.

Ava Chen: agreement on ephemeral handles — cheap metadata, ownership lease. also need clear interface between admission ...

```
DEVICE 0: sm=8.10, tc=available, bf16=available, fp8=unknown
runtime: selected kernel path=matmul_generic
kvcache: fastpath=false
```

text:
Ava Chen: Heads up — noticed a ~3x TTFT spike on /v1/chat around 10:20 UTC after the kernel-selector canary rollout. dashboard: https://console.redwood/team/rt/perf?panel=token_waterfall
Devon Patel: Saw it too. Decode time looks steady, but kernel_time attribution is much higher. Can you drop a token-level waterfall/probe?
Ava Chen: Quick probe dump pasted below:
```
route: /v1/chat model: redwood-7b seq_len: 128
avg_ttft: 240ms (baseline ~80ms)
avg_decode_time: 60ms
avg_kernel_exec: 120ms
avg_kernel_queue: 40ms
kvcache_hit_rate: 0.82
kvcache_fetch_ms (p95): 90ms
selector_branch: microkernel-fallthrough (unexpected)
```
Ravi Singh: That selector_branch stands out — last selector patch added a fallthrough to microkernel for small seqs, but route seq distribution includes many 100+ prompts. Might be mis-evaluating effective seq_len due to tokenization differences.
Sasha Martín: Also se...

title: #eng-runtime: 3419876543
preview:
Priya Patel: Heads-up — after the kv-prefetch tweak landed in canary, we're seeing 2-3x p95 for short sequences (<32 tokens) on nv8p. Latency cliff around 100ms -> 300ms for short-paths. I put a few request traces and flamegraphs in the perf gist. Anyone seen kernel/kvm interactions with prefetch?

Diego Morales: Ye...
text:
Priya Patel: Heads-up — after the kv-prefetch tweak landed in canary, we're seeing 2-3x p95 for short sequences (<32 tokens) on nv8p. Latency cliff around 100ms -> 300ms for short-paths. I put a few request traces and flamegraphs in the perf gist. Anyone seen kernel/kvm interactions with prefetch?

Diego Morales: Yep saw the spike on dashboards. Micro-profiling shows an extra memcpy on the kernel selection hot path when prefetch engages. Batcher tends to coalesce a few short requests into a minislab which then picks a fused-kernel that has higher setup cost.

Ava Chen: Kernel chooser currently looks at seq-length histogram + kv_hit_estimate to pick fused vs fastpath. Prefetch warms KV and increments the "active tokens" accounting, which looks like longer sequences to the selector — could be double-counting KV warmup in the heuristic.

Marta Ivanov: Quick mitigation idea: add a "short-...

title: #eng-runtime: 2365550000
preview:
Jordan Blake: Starting thread to finalize PRD scope for kernel pluggability + KV policy split. Want minimal launch gates and clear ownership.
Priya Rao: Can we commit to splitting into two releases? KV defaults first, kernel selector second. Product needs conservative defaults for tenants.
Marta Gomez: Agreed. KV ro...
text:
Jordan Blake: Starting thread to finalize PRD scope for kernel pluggability + KV policy split. Want minimal launch gates and clear ownership.
Priya Rao: Can we commit to splitting into two releases? KV defaults first, kernel selector second. Product needs conservative defaults for tenants.
Marta Gomez: Agreed. KV rollout items: per-tenant namespace ON by default, memory budget per-tenant, and tenant opt-out API. Add lifecycle key rotation on tenant delete.
Evan Li: For kernel selector, we need a compatibility matrix test and a graceful fallback path. Suggest runtime flag `--kernel-mode=stable|exp` and per-route annotation for opt-in.
Sasha Levin: Acceptance draft: 
- KV: default TTL 30m, LRU eviction, per-tenant memory guardrails, no cross-tenant reads.
- Kernel selector v0: support 2 kernel families, P95 latency <= +10% vs baseline for buckets [8,64,512], generation QA delta < 1.0% o...

title: #eng-runtime: 2248903333
preview:
Ava Chen: OOM spike observed on gpu-pool-3 just now. allocator failed to reserve workspace for batch=8, seq_len=2048 during a KV warmup surge. stack excerpt attached below.

Joel Park: I saw the same. kernel selector jumped to a full-fprop matmul path and memory jumped ~1.8GB. batching heuristic allowed batch growth...
text:
Ava Chen: OOM spike observed on gpu-pool-3 just now. allocator failed to reserve workspace for batch=8, seq_len=2048 during a KV warmup surge. stack excerpt attached below.

Joel Park: I saw the same. kernel selector jumped to a full-fprop matmul path and memory jumped ~1.8GB. batching heuristic allowed batch growth during warmup which made it worse.

Sana R.: Can we do a quick failover: immediate circuit-breaker -> route to quantized-variant + reduce max_batch from 8 to 4 for that pool? That should avoid large workspace allocations.

Joel Park: +1. Also propose a short-term allocator tweak: prefer workspace reuse over fresh reserve when free_mem_pct < 25%. simple config: 
```
allocator: {
 prefer_reuse_threshold_pct: 25,
 max_new_reserve_mb: 512
}
```
This drops 90% of the transient OOMs in my local repro.

Mike L.: Add metric/alert: `allocator.reserve_fail_rate` and `kvcache.miss_bu...

text:
Ava Chen: heads up — around 09:42 UTC we saw p99 jump from ~220ms to ~1.3s on a handful of requests. correlates with a KV cache eviction spike when concurrency hit ~620 on g4-prod pools. not a full outage but SLOs are unhappy. anyone on call?

text:

Ava Chen: ```bash
# repro command
./bench --kernel kgen-v2-archmix-17 --seq 4096 --batch 1 --hw h100 --warm 50 --iter 200
```
Priya Nair: did you check KV cache path? That's where seqlen-specific code paths tend to diverge.
Ava Chen: yes, toggled KV on/off. With KV off the spike disappears. With KV on, perf trace shows extra memcpy in gather step (memcpy3 at gather_kv.c:214).
Samir Gupta: Could be alignment/packing on H1...

preview:

Sam Ortiz: can you paste the profiler slice? memory slope sounds like either KV residency not dropping or...
text:
Ava Chen: heads-up — canary runs on int8-quantized Llama-3 variant show a slow, steady memory increase on long-token sessions ( > 50k tokens). Not a sharp leak, more like a slope. Seen on two different GPU SKUs.
Sam Ortiz: can you paste the profiler slice? memory slope sounds like either KV residency not dropping or fragmentation causing free-list starvation.
Ava Chen: putting a small dump. profiler snapshot at t=75k tokens:
```
KV: total_slots=1_200_000 used_slots=742_312 free_slots=457_688
slab_frag_bytes=34_432_768 free_list_len=3
page_allocs=11245 page_evictions=52
mem_total_gb=28.9 mem_used_gb=23.6 mem_slope_mb_per_min=3.2
quant_mode=INT8 kernel=fast_attn_v3 batch_heuristic=adaptive_lowlat
```
Ava Chen: note slab_frag_bytes >> expected (~8MB baseline). mem_slope for BF16 runs is ~0.7 MB/min under same load.
Liam Patel: suspicious: fast_attn_v3 has a different kv-page alignment/pa...

title: #eng-runtime: 1876543210
preview: Noah Reed: Quick post-RFC sync: proposing two coordinated changes to reduce host fragmentation and cold-page cost: 1) dynamic slab partitioning that adjusts per-tenant slab budgets based on hotness and recent residency, 2) a compressed cold-tier on local NVMe for evicted pages (async compress/decompress). Wants to c...
text:
Noah Reed: Quick post-RFC sync: proposing two coordinated changes to reduce host fragmentation and cold-page cost: 1) dynamic slab partitioning that adjusts per-tenant slab budgets based on hotness and recent residency, 2) a compressed cold-tier on local NVMe for evicted pages (async compress/decompress). Wants to capture tradeoffs before a PR.

Sana Kaur: High-level +1. Questions: how do we avoid CPU compression starving inference? How do we handle rewarm latency when a compressed page is needed synchronously?

Mateo Alvarez: From the prototype: we used zstd-level 3 for compress on-evict (fast) and a small async compressor pool. Benchmarks (canary host A100 80GB + NVMe):
```
compress level=3: ratio ~3.6x, compress cpu ~2.5ms/page, decompress cpu ~0.9ms/page
median rewarm (with async prefetch): +3% p50, p95: +11% when decompression happens on-path
with prefetch-on-evict-hint (keep las...

title: #eng-runtime: 1799954321
preview:

Miguel Torres: Which model/infra/resolutio...
text:
Ava Chen: Ran a focused probe for streaming vs non-streaming using the new kernel-probe flags. Goal: understand kernel selection points, batching heuristics, and KV cache churn under chunked responses. Short summary & raw numbers below, please re-run or comment if you can.

Miguel Torres: Which model/infra/resolution? gpus?

Ava Chen: 2.7B open model on A10G pool (single zone), runtime=release-2026-11. Probe toggled prefer-fused, fallback-matmul, and kernel-switch threshold at token=128. Harness: replay v2 with synthetic client that supports chunking and pacing.

Riley Park: Cool. Did you capture p50/p95/p99 + qps + host cpu?

Miguel Torres: From the primary run (chunk_size=64, no client pacing, warmup 5m): streaming => p50=48ms p95=210ms p99=380ms, qps=1180, host CPU=62%. Non-streaming => p50=65ms p95=320ms p99=560ms, qps=1350, host CPU=44%.

Ava Chen: TL;DR: streaming wins tails but...

- `runtime.kernel.fp8_...
text:
Connor O'Brien: For runtime 1.22.0 we need to lock down *default flag states* (what ships in the release image) + how we document internal overrides (canary/region/model). Can folks weigh in by EOD so release notes + config docs match reality?

Connor O'Brien: Flags in scope (from the sprint):
- `runtime.kernel.fp8_attention.enabled`
- `runtime.kernel.int8_matmul.enabled`
- `runtime.kernel.autoselect_fp8_int8.enabled`
- `runtime.scheduler.mixed_prefill_decode_v2.enabled`
- `runtime.perf_canary.gate_quant_kernels.enabled`
- `runtime.metrics.tagging.model_gpu_kernel_mode.enabled`

Hiro Tanaka: For kernel side, my strong preference is:
- ship binaries with the code paths present
- keep `fp8_attention` + `int8_matmul` default OFF
- `autoselect_fp8_int8` default OFF as well, otherwise we’re effectively turning on kernels via heuristic.

Hiro Tanaka: We can still enable per model/GPU/region...

title: #eng-runtime: 1776451055
preview:

text:
Talia Benson: need to lock canary plan today — which regions first + who’s on point for watch/rollback. goal: enable new kernel paths behind flags for a small cohort before we touch broader hosted traffic.

Rafael Mendes: from SRE side: prefer a region w/ good observability + easy rollback + minimal enterprise sensitivity. I’d start w/ us-west (lowest enterprise SLAs impact for our current cohorts) then us-east. I’d avoid eu-west as first step given last week’s noise + that SUP ticket.

Grace Kim: +1 avoid eu-west first. the TTFT regression we saw was in a subset of prompts but it’ll be hard to debug w/ EU variance/driver skew.

Allison Grant: dashboards can slice by region/model/kernel-mode now (post pr-19934), so any region is “watchable”. but agree eu-west is historically noisier due to mixed fleet images.

Hiro Tanaka: for canary cohort do we want FP8-only first, or FP8+INT8 toget...

text:
Grace Kim: seeing a TTFT regression in a subset of prompts w/ FP8 attention enabled (flag `runtime.kernels.fp8_attention=true`). Not everywhere, but enough to fail the nightly perf gate on H100.

Grace Kim: quick stats from matrix runner (top-hosted-suite-v3, H100, model=“mistral-7b-instruct”):
- baseline TTFT p50: ~235ms, p95: ~410ms
- FP8-attn TTFT p50: ~245ms (+4%), p95: ~515ms (+25%)
TPOT looks basically flat / slightly better, so it’s mostly prefill.

Grace Kim: the regressing cases are mostly short prompts + tool/structured variant (few hundred tokens prefill), smaller batch sizes (b<=4). Hypothesis: kernel launch overhead / scheduler mix causing prefill fragmentation.

Hiro Tanaka: :eyes: thanks. do we have the breakdown by prefill vs decode time from the new scheduler metrics? (PR-19855)

Julian Moore: yeah, I can pull from the `scheduler_prefill_ms` histogram. For the failing...

text:
Noah Patel: Kicking off eu-west expansion for perf-canary. Goal: get canary running + baselines aligned before we flip alerts on. 

Proposed steps:
1) deploy canary in eu-west in observe-only (no warn/page)
2) run 24–48h synthetic workload, validate metric parity w/ us-east
3) baseline gen job writes eu-west artifacts (SKU-aware)
4) compare baseline deltas + variance, decide thresholds
5) enable warning alerts, then paging after 1 week if clean

Need owners to confirm: model list, SKU, and dashboard links. :eyes:

Vanessa Ortiz: eu-west fleet is mixed right now: mostly H100-80GB, but there’s still a small A100-80GB pool on Dedicated. For hosted it’s H100 only. If canary fans out across both, baselines will be messy.

Noah Patel: +1. Let’s pin canary targets to hosted tier first (H100). Dedicated expansion later once we’ve got region+SKU thresholds.

Sofia Ivanova: Agree. Also need to ...

title: #eng-runtime: 1771234567
preview:
Omar Rahman: Heads up — after the kernel-selection change that prefers the new FP4-sparse matmul for short sequences, we saw a ~25% throughput drop and intermittent memory spikes on prod-gpu-pool-3 around 06:42 UTC. graph: https://grafana.internal/r/xyz :eyes:
Priya Desai: ugh. do we have profiler traces? is this is...
text:
Omar Rahman: Heads up — after the kernel-selection change that prefers the new FP4-sparse matmul for short sequences, we saw a ~25% throughput drop and intermittent memory spikes on prod-gpu-pool-3 around 06:42 UTC. graph: https://grafana.internal/r/xyz :eyes:
Priya Desai: ugh. do we have profiler traces? is this isolated to NF4 vs FP4 or the kernel switch path?
Omar Rahman: I grabbed a quick pprof on one affected node. attaching snippet below. high memcpy counts and sudden allocations in kv_cache codepath.
Alice Chen: paste of profiler (truncated):
```
CPU: 0.8s total
memcpy: 320ms
kernel_fp4_sparse_matmul: 180ms
kv_cache_flush: 90ms
alloc: 250MB spikes at request start
```
Noticed frequent kv_cache->evict cycles at ~1.2s intervals when sequence length > 512.
Mateo Alvarez: that eviction pattern smells like batching heuristic holding onto KV pages until a full batch triggers, then we...

title: #eng-runtime: 1770974409
preview:

Noah Patel: specifically:
- prefill vs decode time split (runtime-only)

text:
Noah Patel: want to propose we add a couple attribution metrics to perf-canary + runtime telemetry. right now when we see p95 up or tok/s down we’re still guessing if it’s prefill vs decode vs scheduler vs batching.

Noah Patel: specifically:
- prefill vs decode time split (runtime-only)
- batch depth / effective batch size during decode
- maybe “tokens per request” + “prefill tokens” so we can normalize shapes

Sofia Ivanova: +1. queueing_time helps, but last incident we couldn’t tell if tok/s drop was due to smaller batches or slower kernel.

Sofia Ivanova: for batching depth, can we expose something like: avg_active_sequences, avg_batch_size, and distribution? tail matters.

Omar El-Sayed: we have some of this internally in scheduler debug counters, but not exported in a stable way.

Omar El-Sayed: what’s the minimum set you want on the canary side vs globally in runtime metrics?

...

title: #eng-runtime: 1770805606
preview:
Noah Patel: trying to reconcile a thing from last night: microbench suite shows ~8-10% tokens/sec drop on llama-3.1-70b (H100) after the kernel change, but perf-canary e2e is basically flat (maybe +2% p95, within noise). are we just measuring different worlds or missing a signal?

Hiro Tanaka: different worlds mostl...
text:
Noah Patel: trying to reconcile a thing from last night: microbench suite shows ~8-10% tokens/sec drop on llama-3.1-70b (H100) after the kernel change, but perf-canary e2e is basically flat (maybe +2% p95, within noise). are we just measuring different worlds or missing a signal?

Hiro Tanaka: different worlds mostly. microbench is single-stream, no scheduler contention, fixed seq lens. canary is going through full stack (queue + batch + cache), and our scheduler changes can hide kernel slowdowns by shifting batch composition.

Sofia Ivanova: +1. e2e includes queueing + continuous batching effects. also: microbench uses decode-heavy (1 token prefill, long decode) in some tests. canary prompt distro is more mixed. a kernel regression might be isolated to specific (prefill, seq_len) combos.

Rina Kobayashi: fwiw the microbench “decode_2048” case is the one that regressed hardest. our ca...

title: #eng-runtime: 1770383607
preview:
Noah Patel: seeing a big gap between staging and prod variance on the canary runs. staging p95 is basically flat, prod p95 is bouncing way more. before we lock thresholds, can we sanity check what “normal” looks like in prod?

Rina Kobayashi: +1. staging harness run from this morning (same seed + same shapes) had p9...
text:
Noah Patel: seeing a big gap between staging and prod variance on the canary runs. staging p95 is basically flat, prod p95 is bouncing way more. before we lock thresholds, can we sanity check what “normal” looks like in prod?

Rina Kobayashi: +1. staging harness run from this morning (same seed + same shapes) had p95 within ~2-3% across 6h. prod shadow in us-east was ~10-15% swing depending on hour.

Sofia Ivanova: prod has real queueing and batch dynamics that staging doesn’t. if we set thresholds from staging variance we’re gonna page constantly.

Omar El-Sayed: do we have the breakdown on where variance comes from? e2e vs runtime-only? (i.e., queue + scheduler vs kernel execution)

Nadia Rahman: I pulled a quick compare. Same model + same canary profile.
- staging: runtime_only_p95_ms stddev ~1.8ms on a mean ~62ms
- prod (us-east): runtime_only_p95_ms stddev ~4-5ms on a mean ~65ms
...

Marcus Reed: Perf summary (...
text:
Talia Benson: GA check for kernel auto-selection v2 (runtime 1.21): can we confirm all GA criteria met + get final approvals? Looking for: perf deltas, error rates, denylist status, SLO gates, and rollout readiness. If you’re a required approver, pls reply with ✅ / 🚫 + any last caveats.

Marcus Reed: Perf summary (Hosted canary + perf-suite):
- p95 latency: -6.8% overall weighted across canary scenarios (biggest win on A100 + short/med decode)
- p99 latency: -0.9% overall; within gate (no sustained tail regressions)
- Throughput: +4.1% tokens/s at same concurrency; variance down (less jitter in mixed workloads)
- Long-context (16k/32k) after ENG fix (PR-28522): no regression vs v1 in latest run; p99 recovered.
Numbers are in the perf results sheet + perf-canary dashboards. ✅ from perf side.

Omar El-Sayed: Tail latency + errors from observability:
- Error rate: flat. No increase in t...

title: #eng-runtime: 1769968421
preview:

Lena Fischer: :wave: I’ll drive the design doc + proposed policy (two-queue + aging). Need i...
text:
Sofia Ivanova: Kickoff thread for mixed-workload scheduler prototype. Goal is to get initial short/long thresholds + owners so Lena can start the design doc. If you’ve looked at traces already, drop your numbers/links here.

Lena Fischer: :wave: I’ll drive the design doc + proposed policy (two-queue + aging). Need input on (a) classification threshold(s) and (b) what we can reliably compute at enqueue time (prompt tokens vs predicted prefill cost). Also who’s owning benchmarks/trace replay?

Claire Dubois: I pulled a quick distro from last week’s anonymized Hosted chat traces (sample ~2M req). Roughly: median prompt ~120 tok, p90 ~700, p95 ~1.6k, p99 ~6.2k. Long tail is heavy. My take: “short” cutoff somewhere 256–384 toks, and maybe treat >2048 as definitely long.

Claire Dubois: Link to working sheet (WIP): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1rwi-mixed-scheduler (see “mixed-work...

title: #eng-runtime: 1769877202
preview:
Noah Patel: quick q for perf-canary attribution: should we add a KV cache hit-rate signal to the canary metrics? thinking it might help explain p95 shifts where runtime-only looks flat but e2e latency moves (or vice versa).

Sofia Ivanova: do you mean KV cache like prefix/KV reuse across requests? (not the per-reque...
text:
Noah Patel: quick q for perf-canary attribution: should we add a KV cache hit-rate signal to the canary metrics? thinking it might help explain p95 shifts where runtime-only looks flat but e2e latency moves (or vice versa).

Sofia Ivanova: do you mean KV cache like prefix/KV reuse across requests? (not the per-request kv cache that’s always there)

Noah Patel: yeah, the cross-request prefix cache / reuse. the one that gets better/worse depending on cache policy + eviction behavior.

Omar El-Sayed: technically we can expose it. runtime already knows when a request had a cache hit vs miss, plus how many tokens reused.

Hiro Tanaka: what’s the hypothesis? regressions we’ve seen from cache changes have been mostly memory pressure -> more evictions -> queueing/batching changes, not direct kernel perf.

Noah Patel: exactly. today canary screams “p95 up” and we’re guessing: scheduler? infra?...

title: #eng-runtime: 1769455201
preview:
Noah Patel: Want to lock this down today: what’s the *hard* GPU overhead budget for perf-canary per region + the QPS caps we’re enforcing? Also need a clear “disable switch” if we see any unexpected load.

Sofia Ivanova: For design doc we had <0.5% GPU capacity per region for covered models. Are we sticking to that ...
text:
Noah Patel: Want to lock this down today: what’s the *hard* GPU overhead budget for perf-canary per region + the QPS caps we’re enforcing? Also need a clear “disable switch” if we see any unexpected load.

Sofia Ivanova: For design doc we had <0.5% GPU capacity per region for covered models. Are we sticking to that or going more conservative for phase 1?

Sean Gallagher: +1 keep it conservative initially. SRE expectation: canary should be *invisible* to customer SLOs. If we get into a situation where canary contributes to queueing, we need a kill switch that doesn’t require waiting on deploys.

Vanessa Ortiz: From fleet side: us-east pool has some tighter headroom during peak. I’d prefer we budget against *peak* not average. 0.5% is ok if it’s real cap, not “best effort”.

Noah Patel: Ok, proposal:
- Phase 1 (us-east, 2-3 models): target 0.25% GPU overhead, hard cap 0.5%
- Phase 2+ (m...

text:
Sofia Ivanova: quick sanity check request — can someone help validate the scheduler queueing-time metric under load? I’m seeing queue_time_p95 jump but E2E p95 is flat, which feels… off.

Omar El-Sayed: which metric + labels are you looking at?

Sofia Ivanova: `runtime.scheduler.queue_time_ms` (histogram) split by model + region. Dashboard panel in the new canary view is “Queueing (p95)”.

Noah Patel: is that from canary traffic only or all traffic?

Sofia Ivanova: canary only. label `source=perf-canary`.

Logan Wright: fwiw we changed the instrumentation boundary in the last runtime PR — we should verify definition is still “enqueue -> begin_execution” and not “enqueue -> first_token”.

Omar El-Sayed: yeah definition should be:
- start: request accepted by scheduler and placed into pending queue
- end: batch is formed and request is handed to execution (prefill)
NOT including GPU com...

title: #eng-runtime: 1769007005
preview:
Omar El-Sayed: heads up: I think we have a bug in the runtime export for prefill/decode token split. Seeing `prefill_tokens` ~= `total_tokens` on a bunch of streaming requests, which is impossible unless decode=0.

Sofia Ivanova: where are you seeing it? grafana or raw events?

Omar El-Sayed: raw telemetry rows (sta...
text:
Omar El-Sayed: heads up: I think we have a bug in the runtime export for prefill/decode token split. Seeing `prefill_tokens` ~= `total_tokens` on a bunch of streaming requests, which is impossible unless decode=0.

Sofia Ivanova: where are you seeing it? grafana or raw events?

Omar El-Sayed: raw telemetry rows (stage breakdown). Example request_id `req_8f2c...`:
- total_tokens=842
- prefill_tokens=842
- decode_tokens=811
So prefill+decode > total. That’s what’s feeding cost-model breakdown.

Grace Kim: oof. that looks like we’re emitting per-stage counters as cumulative instead of delta.

Logan Wright: +1, we saw a sudden jump in `rw.runtime.tokens.prefill` at ~02/18 21:00 UTC when the new exporter started rolling. Drift SLI got noisy but totals still kinda matched because billing is using total_tokens.

Nadia Rahman: confirming: the canonical token meter has total prompt+completion ...

title: #eng-runtime: 1768793205
preview:
Sofia Ivanova: ok folks, final review thread for batching tier defaults before we cut runtime-1.22. Please sanity check the knob sets + call out remaining known issues we should document.

Sofia Ivanova: context: we reconciled the fast-tier p99 regression from last week + merged the cardinality hotfix. now we need t...
text:
Sofia Ivanova: ok folks, final review thread for batching tier defaults before we cut runtime-1.22. Please sanity check the knob sets + call out remaining known issues we should document.

Sofia Ivanova: context: we reconciled the fast-tier p99 regression from last week + merged the cardinality hotfix. now we need to lock:
- defaults per tier (fast / standard / cost-efficient)
- any model-family constraints (70B vs 8B)
- overload behavior + guardrails

Sofia Ivanova: proposed FINAL values (decoder-only families; applies to Llama3.x, Mistral, Qwen2 unless noted)

FAST
- max_wait_ms: 2
- max_batch_tokens_total: 2048
- max_batch_requests: 8
- prefill_concurrency_limit: 2
- decode_step_tokens_cap: 256 (cap per tick to reduce long-decode hogging)
- admission_control: on
 - queue_soft_limit: 24
 - queue_hard_limit: 40 (reject/429)
- anti_starvation: on (age-based bump after 80ms)

STANDARD
...

title: #eng-runtime: 1768690388
preview:
Hiro Tanaka: we need better attribution on perf regressions. can we tag *which* kernel got picked + runtime build id in telemetry? right now canary says “p95 up 18%” and we spend 2h guessing if it’s scheduler vs kernel vs fleet.

Noah Patel: +1. for perf-canary we can include these in the alert payload too (so incid...
text:
Hiro Tanaka: we need better attribution on perf regressions. can we tag *which* kernel got picked + runtime build id in telemetry? right now canary says “p95 up 18%” and we spend 2h guessing if it’s scheduler vs kernel vs fleet.

Noah Patel: +1. for perf-canary we can include these in the alert payload too (so incident-bot shows it).

Omar El-Sayed: runtime already knows kernel selection internally, but we don’t export it today. question is what granularity we can expose without blowing up cardinality.

Logan Wright: ^ yep. if we put full kernel name per request as a label, prometheus is gonna have a bad time. we should aim for stable, low-cardinality tags.

Sofia Ivanova: what do we actually need to answer quickly?
- did kernel implementation change (new fused attention variant, etc)
- did selection policy change (picked different kernel for same shape)
- did runtime build / flags ch...

title: #eng-runtime: 1768604412
preview: Nadia Rahman: flagging an issue we’re gonna hit w/ perf-canary baselines: baseline drift over weeks as fleet mix / traffic mix changes slowly. if we keep comparing to a fixed baseline, we’ll eventually trigger “regressions” even though nothing broke. conversely if baseline tracks too fast, we can mask real regressio...
text:
Nadia Rahman: flagging an issue we’re gonna hit w/ perf-canary baselines: baseline drift over weeks as fleet mix / traffic mix changes slowly. if we keep comparing to a fixed baseline, we’ll eventually trigger “regressions” even though nothing broke. conversely if baseline tracks too fast, we can mask real regressions. thoughts on the right strategy?

Noah Patel: +1. we already saw it in staging when we started running more long-context shapes at night. p95 “regressed” vs baseline but it was just prompt bucket mix shifting.

Sofia Ivanova: drift sources I’m seeing:
- hardware mix: A100 pool gradually replaced w/ H100 in us-east
- scheduler behavior: batch size distribution shifts w/ QPS
- workload shape: prompt buckets shift w/ product traffic + time of day
- kernel auto-selection: minor change in seq-len distribution changes chosen kernel

Logan Wright: from telemetry side, the bigge...

title: #eng-runtime: 1768512097
preview:
Sofia Ivanova: pulling in folks from cost-model thread. question from @Ethan Park/@Logan Wright: which batching metrics can we commit to as *stable* for v1 cost-model contract? we have like 8 different "batch size" numbers floating around :grimacing:

Ethan Park: yep. we need something that won’t churn every runtime...
text:
Sofia Ivanova: pulling in folks from cost-model thread. question from @Ethan Park/@Logan Wright: which batching metrics can we commit to as *stable* for v1 cost-model contract? we have like 8 different "batch size" numbers floating around :grimacing:

Ethan Park: yep. we need something that won’t churn every runtime release. goal is to model batch efficiency + queue time in cost breakdown. what can we reliably export per-request and/or per-batch?

Logan Wright: +1. telemetry wants a few canonical fields with clear semantics + units. otherwise we’re stuck with free-text tags.

Omar El-Sayed: ok, current state:
- scheduler builds a "batch" object, but it can change between prefill and decode
- continuous batching means a request can join a decode wave after initial prefill
- we also have chunking on long prompts (prefill can be split)
so if you ask for "batch_size" without specifying st...
